sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 57:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Topics 57:1
items.html: In the second departure, when ZON rose to Malchut of the Rosh, two couplings were incorporated there, similar to the ascents of ZON after first departure. Here, too, the female received illumination of the upper Keter in the first coupling, but the difference is that here, the degrees descended and there was light of Hochma in the vessel of the upper Keter.
